title: Arm Sling
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- For the comfort and support of suspected wrist, arm, or shoulder fractures, or soft tissue injuries.

#### Procedure

Figure 1

Figure 2

Figure 3

- If required, administer pain relief prior to applying splint and/or arm sling.

- Confirm distal pulses are present.

- Apply cardboard splint if required.

- Remove triangular bandage from packaging.

- Position bandage underneath the patient’s injured arm with the “pointed” end at the elbow of the injured arm and the “tying” end over the shoulder of the uninjured arm (see **Figure 1**).

- Fold the other half of the bandage over the injured arm so that the “tying” end is positioned over the shoulder of the injured arm. (see **Figure 2**).

- Secure both tying ends in a knot behind the patient’s neck/behind the shoulder of the uninjured arm at a level of comfort for the patient (see **Figure 3**).

- Reconfirm distal pulses are present, sensation, and capillary refill.
